verse_text: His winnowing fan is in His hand, and He will thoroughly clean out His threshing floor, and gather the wheat into His barn; but the chaff He will burn with unquenchable fire.'

links.context: Christ baptizes in the fire of the Holy Spirit, the power and grace of God divinely poured out on all believers at baptism. (See also fire in v. 10; note at [[LUK.3:16]], [[LUK.3:17]].) In John's culture, a slave would carry the king's sandals; thus John declares himself to be even lower than a slave of Jesus. John's inability to carry Christ's sandal has a second meaning, for carrying another's sandal once meant taking someone else's responsibility ([[RUT.4:7]]). Here it shows that John could not have carried the responsibility that Christ carries, and that the Law could not redeem the world as Christ has come to do.
